input.background: A parallel system consists of 3 independent components, each having 0.95 reliability. The system reliability is calculated using the formula $$R=1-(1-0.95)^3=0.9999$$.
input.question: How can the mean time to failure (MTTF) of the system be calculated?

output.answer: The mean time to failure of the system can be obtained as $$M_{tbf}=\int_0^{\infty} R(t) dt=\int_0^{\infty}\left(1-\prod_{i=1}^n\left(1-e^{-\lambda_i t}\right)\right) dt$$. After simplifications, $$M_{tbf}=\sum_{i=1}^n \sum_{j_i=1}^n \sum_{\substack{j_{i-1}=1 \\ \left\{j_1>j_2>\ldots>j_i\right\}}}^n \ldots \sum_{j_2=1}^n \sum_{j_1=1}^n \frac{(-1)^{i+1}}{\left(\sum_{k=1}^i \lambda_{j_k}\right)}$$.
